living-concrete.pattern: Before designing, list every concept that must be represented. For CLI: operations, flags, outputs. Enumerate then map.
Each implementation element serves exactly one purpose. Commands don't overlap. Flags don't duplicate. Minimal surface area, maximum coverage.
BentOS WHO -> --who flag BentOS WHERE -> --spacetime flag Every ontological concept has syntactic expression.
Table: rows = concepts, columns = implementations. Every row has exactly one column filled. Empty row = gap. Multiple columns = overlap.
Implementation links to design links to requirement. Trace any line of code to its justification.
living-concrete.antipattern: "This feels right" without explicit justification. Every choice must answer "why this and not alternatives?"
Concepts in implementation but not enumerated. If not in the list, shouldn't be in the code.
